channel.title: Global Sanctions News (Custom RSS)
channel.description: Auto-generated feed from rendered Global Sanctions news list

item.title: CJEU rejects Ammar Sharif’s sanctions delisting appeal
item.description: The Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) has dismissed Ammar Sharif's appeal against the General Court's judgment in Case T‑503/23, which rejected his challenge to his 2023 relisting under th

item.title: UK issues licence authorising insolvency-related payments connected with the Prince Group and Lukoil-related FAQ
item.description: The UK has amended General Licence INT/2026/9491628 which allowed insolvency-related payments and activities connected with UK-sanctioned Prince Group and their subsidiaries – publication notice. T

item.title: Chinese Ministry of Commerce announces reporting mechanism for violations of export controls on strategic minerals
item.description: The Chinese Ministry of Commerce has announced a new procedure for reporting violations of export control regulations concerning dual-use items derived from strategic minerals. The procedure will be e

item.title: EU Sanctions Helpdesk publishes guidance on sanctions compliance
item.description: The EU Sanctions Helpdesk has published guidance for businesses to mitigate their geographic exposure to sanctions. The guidance encourages businesses to be alert to: The transit route of goods,

item.title: US and UK publish comparative review of their sanctions enforcement bodies
item.description: The US and the UK held their annual strategic dialogue on their Enhanced Partnership on sanctions in January 2026. To improve the private sector’s understanding of sanctions administration, the UK and

item.title: US issues CCU Commercial Bank wind down general license
item.description: The US has issued Transnational Criminal Organisation-related General License No.2, authorising certain wind down transactions which are otherwise prohibited by the Transnational Criminal Organisation

item.title: US adds 35 linked to cyber scam network to transnational criminal organisation sanctions list
item.description: The US has added 9 people and 26 entities to its Specially Designated Nationals and Blocked Persons (SDN) List, for being linked to the Prince Group Transnational Criminal Organisation – Press Release

item.title: US designates Cuba’s revenue generation network and removes 4 from Russia sanctions list
item.description: The US has added 5 entities and 1 person to the Specially Designated Nationals and Blocked Persons (SDN) List – Press Release. The designations were made under Executive Order 14404, which targets peo

item.title: EU updates South Sudan minister’s travel ban
item.description: The EU has updated its statement of reasons for listing Michael Makuei Leuth under Regulation (EU) 2015/735, the EU’s South Sudan sanctions list. Mr Leuth was South Sudan’s Minister for Information an

item.title: UK launches parliamentary inquiry into deterring Russian aggression – evidence by 8 July
item.description: The UK has launched a parliamentary inquiry on the UK’s approach to deterring Russian aggression, with a focus on imposing costs and constraints – press release. Parliamentary inquiries are convene
